sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the initial state:
Green single container (initial, rows 9-11, cols 27-32):
```
BBGNNNNN
BBGNNNrN
BBGNNNNN
```
So it's a 3x6 container with:
- G wall on left (col 27)
- N shape (cols 28-32) - a full N shape (11 pixels)
- r red dot at (10, 31)
Yellow single container (initial, rows 27-34, cols 9-13):
```
BBBGGGBBB
BBBYYYBBB
BBBYYYBBB
BBBYYYBBB
BBBYYYBBB
BBBYYYBBB
BBBYYYBBB
BBBYrYBBB
```
So it's an 8x5 container with:
- G wall on top (row 27)
- Y shape (rows 28-34) - a full Y shape (19 pixels)
- r red dot at (34, 11)
Now the current state:
Green single container (current, rows 9-11, cols 27-31):
```
BBGNNBBB
BBGrNBBB
BBGNNBBB
```
So it's a 3x4 container with:
- G wall on left (col 27)
- N shape (cols 28-31) - a shrunk N shape (7 pixels)
- r red dot at (10, 29)
Yellow single container (current, rows 27-29, cols 9-13):
```
BBBGGGBBB
BBBYrYBBB
BBBYYYBBB
```
So it's a 3x5 container with:
- G wall on top (row 27)
- Y shape (rows 28-29) - a shrunk Y shape (5 pixels)
- r red dot at (28, 11)
So the single containers have shrunk from their initial state. The green container went from 3x6 to 3x4 (N shape from 11 to 7 pixels), and the yellow container went from 8x5 to 3x5 (Y shape from 19 to 5 pixels).
Now, the key insight is that clicking the U-shapes in the double containers toggles the single containers. When I click a U-shape in the green double container, the green single container toggles between full and shrunk. When I click a U-shape in the yellow double container, the yellow single container toggles between full and shrunk.
But I've tried all 4 combinations and none completed the level. So maybe the goal is not about the container states.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the goal is to click on the red dots inside the single containers. The green single container has a red dot at (10, 29) and the yellow single container has a red dot at (28, 11). Let me try clicking these.
Actually, I already clicked on the yellow single container's red dot at (31, 10) earlier, but that was when the container was full. Now the container is shrunk, so the red dot is at (28, 11). Let me try clicking at (28, 11).
But wait, I also need to check if the red dots are part of the N/Y shapes or separate objects. Looking at the segmentation, I see:
- Node 2: N, 5 pixels - green U-shape in single container (current)
- Node 3: r, 1 pixel - red dot in green container
- Node 16: Y, 5 pixels - yellow U-shape in single container (current)
- Node 17: r, 1 pixel - red dot in yellow container
So the red dots are separate 1-pixel objects. Let me try clicking on them.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try clicking the red dots inside the single containers
# Green single container red dot at (10, 29)
# Yellow single container red dot at (28, 11)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 29}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
